items.title: The Anticipated Relational Effects of Confronting Bias (or not) in Interracial Friendships
items.author: Ashley L. Berkebile-Weinberg, Riana M. Brown, Casey E. McMahon, Maureen A. Craig
items.content: <article>Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Most biased comments people experience are from friends. However, little is known about how people process experiences in which a friend expresses bias and how the relationship might be affected. The current research examines the anticipated relational ...</article>
